description_long: This is a floatplane with a high-wing monoplane design, featuring a pair of large pontoons for water landings and takeoffs. The fuselage is streamlined and painted primarily white with red accents on the wingtips, tail, and pontoons. The aircraft has a twin-boom tail structure with three vertical stabilizers, giving it a distinctive H-tail configuration. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating one or two seats. The wings are braced with struts and wires for added structural support, and there are two visible propeller engines mounted on the wings. The overall design suggests a utility or recreational seaplane suitable for operations in urban waterfronts or lakeside environments.
description: A high-wing floatplane with twin-boom H-tail, red accents, and dual propeller engines mounted on the wings. Features large pontoons for water landings and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-wing, twin-boom tail, and large pontoons beneath the fuselage, with red accents on the wingtips and tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide wingspan, dual propeller engines, and the pontoons extending below the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the twin-boom tail, pontoons, and wing structure with visible struts.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration with three vertical stabilizers and the full span of the wings and pontoons.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with straight wings, supported by struts and wires, no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin booms and three vertical stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two large pontoons for water operations.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit at the front of the fuselage, likely single or dual-seat with visible canopy.
